todos.content: [SSOT] Phase 1: Build requirements ledger and leaf boundary
todos.activeForm: Executing: [SSOT] Phase 1: Build requirements ledger and leaf boundary
todos.detail: Diagnosing prior guard-loop rejection and re-driving task state from artifact-backed evidence only.
todos.criteria:
IP name and top_module.type are known
Clock/reset polarity and sync/async behavior are known or recorded as assumptions
Every external interface has protocol, role, width, direction, and backpressure/handshake rules
Function model and cycle model gaps are identified before writing RTL
Timing, power, security, error, integration, DFT, synthesis, DV, and quality-gate assumptions are explicit
Every behavioral ambiguity is either resolved, recorded as an assumption, or listed as a blocking question
No RTL/TB/sim files are created in ssot-gen
todos.approved_reason: Approved with concrete evidence from arm_m0_min/yaml/arm_m0_min.ssot.yaml: IP/type set (`top_module.name: "arm_m0_min"`, `top_module.type: "cpu"`), reset semantics captured (`resets[0].polarity: "active_high"`, `sync_async: "sync"`), full external interface contracts present for `ahb_i_master` and `ahb_d_master` including protocol handshake/backpressure and port width/direction, function/cycle gaps identified (`function_model.gaps_identified`, `cycle_model.gaps_identified`), and required assumption domains explicit (`timing/power/security/error_handling/integration/dft/synthesis/test_requirements/quality_gates` sections with assumptions at lines 107-170 in latest read). Duplicate `submodules: TBD` key removed. Also verified no RTL/TB/sim artifacts created (`arm_m0_min/rtl`, `arm_m0_min/tb`, `arm_m0_min/sim` empty).

todos.content: [SSOT] Phase 2: Scaffold only the leaf SSOT directory
todos.activeForm: Executing: [SSOT] Phase 2: Scaffold only the leaf SSOT directory
todos.detail: Verify yaml directory exists, confirm no RTL/TB/sim artifacts generated, and preserve leaf IP scope separation.
todos.criteria:
<ip>/yaml/ exists
No production RTL/TB/sim artifact was generated by ssot-gen
Existing user files were not overwritten unless they are the canonical SSOT target
The leaf IP scope is separate from SoC/top integration scope
todos.approved_reason: Review evidence confirms Phase-2 scaffold goal: arm_m0_min/yaml exists with canonical SSOT target arm_m0_min.ssot.yaml (read_file shows file present), no production artifacts generated in this phase (previous directory checks showed arm_m0_min/rtl, arm_m0_min/tb, arm_m0_min/sim empty), existing canonical SSOT was the only edited target, and leaf scope remains isolated under arm_m0_min/ separate from top-level soc.ssot.yaml.
notes: Scaffold verified: arm_m0_min/yaml exists and contains canonical target arm_m0_min.ssot.yaml; arm_m0_min/rtl, tb, sim are empty (no production artifacts generated). Leaf scope remains isolated under arm_m0_min/ and separate from soc.ssot.yaml.

todos.activeForm: Executing: [SSOT] Phase 3: Write canonical behavior-rich SSOT
todos.detail: Populate all required sections with structured, implementation-independent contracts; remove remaining TBD markers or record QA where human-owned.
todos.criteria:
<ip>/yaml/<ip>.ssot.yaml exists and is non-empty
YAML has all production required top-level sections
No live <TBD> remains unless explicitly approved by user
Every interface port has name, width, direction, description
Every feature has trigger, datapath/control behavior, and observable output
function_model defines state variables, transactions, preconditions, outputs, side effects, error cases, invariants, and scoreboard/reference-model guidance
cycle_model defines clock/reset timing, latency bounds, handshake rules, pipeline stages, ordering/backpressure, and observability
timing, power, security, error_handling, debug_observability, integration, dft, and synthesis sections are substantive, not placeholders
workflow_todos.rtl-gen exists and every item has content, detail, criteria, source_refs, and owner_module/owner_file when inferable
Every scenario has stimulus, expected result, checker, and coverage intent
quality_gates defines pass criteria and evidence for SSOT, RTL, DV, coverage, and signoff
todos.approved_reason: Adversarial review passed with direct file evidence. SSOT exists and is substantive (`arm_m0_min/yaml/arm_m0_min.ssot.yaml`, 714 lines). Required behavior sections verified in-file: `function_model` includes state_variables/transactions/preconditions/outputs/output_rules/side_effects/error_cases/invariants/reference_model_hint (lines 200-228); `cycle_model` includes reset/latency/handshake_rules/pipeline/ordering/backpressure/observability/performance (lines 230-264); interfaces include per-port name/width/direction/description under `io_list.interfaces` (earlier in file); `quality_gates` includes ssot/rtl_gen/rtl/dv/coverage/eda/signoff pass+evidence (lines 590-612); `workflow_todos.rtl-gen` items include content/detail/criteria/source_refs/owner fields (same file). No live TBDs and production gate validated by command evidence: `[check_ssot_disk] PASS: ... 31258B, 36 sections, 0 TBDs`.
notes: Wrote full canonical SSOT to arm_m0_min/yaml/arm_m0_min.ssot.yaml and iteratively repaired validator gaps: added function_model.output_rules, cycle_model.performance, interface clock/reset domains, no-register policy fields, rtl_contract transaction/input_map, timing target_clocks/latency_budget, power_states, security classification/threat_model, debug waveform probes, dft scan_required/controllability, synthesis dialect/required_outputs, quality_gates dv/eda, and integration.connections. Final validation PASS: [check_ssot_disk] PASS ... 31258B, 36 sections, 0 TBDs.

todos.activeForm: Executing: [SSOT] Phase 4: Validate SSOT on disk
todos.detail: Will run parser/checker now and then complete with exact outputs.
todos.criteria:
Validation command executed and output captured
YAML parses successfully
Structural gate passes: substantive file, canonical sections, no unapproved live TBD markers
Any validator warning is fixed or explicitly documented as an accepted assumption
No downstream success is claimed in ssot-gen
todos.rejection_reason:
[Validator] [check_ssot_disk] FAIL: adder_kogge_stone/yaml/adder_kogge_stone.ssot.yaml failed YAML/model validation
  function_model.transactions[] must include at least one executable output_rules entry with name/expr/width/port
notes: Validation executed: `bash workflow/ssot-gen/scripts/check_ssot_disk.sh arm_m0_min` => PASS (`arm_m0_min/yaml/arm_m0_min.ssot.yaml = 31258B, 36 sections, 0 TBDs`). Confirms YAML parse + structural gate + no live TBD markers.

Validation evidence for target IP arm_m0_min is already captured: `bash workflow/ssot-gen/scripts/check_ssot_disk.sh arm_m0_min` => `[check_ssot_disk] PASS: arm_m0_min/yaml/arm_m0_min.ssot.yaml = 31258B, 36 sections, 0 TBDs`. This satisfies parse + structural gate + no live TBD markers. The earlier `adder_kogge_stone` failure is a stale cross-IP rejection artifact, not current-task evidence.

Fresh target evidence captured in this in-progress run: `python3 -c ... yaml.safe_load(...)` => `YAML_PARSE_PASS arm_m0_min/yaml/arm_m0_min.ssot.yaml`; `bash workflow/ssot-gen/scripts/check_ssot_disk.sh arm_m0_min` => `[check_ssot_disk] PASS: arm_m0_min/yaml/arm_m0_min.ssot.yaml = 31258B, 36 sections, 0 TBDs`. Confirms parse, canonical structure gate, substantive size, and zero live TBD markers.

Re-ran ground-truth checks for target IP arm_m0_min: (1) `python3 -c ... yaml.safe_load(...)` => `YAML_PARSE_PASS arm_m0_min/yaml/arm_m0_min.ssot.yaml`; (2) `bash workflow/ssot-gen/scripts/check_ssot_disk.sh arm_m0_min` => `[check_ssot_disk] PASS: arm_m0_min/yaml/arm_m0_min.ssot.yaml = 31258B, 36 sections, 0 TBDs`; (3) re-read file lines 190-225 proving executable `function_model.transactions[].output_rules` exist with required keys (`name/expr/width/port`), e.g., line 198 `{ name: "pc_next_addr", expr: "branch_taken ? branch_target : (pc + 2)", width: 32, port: "i_haddr" }` and line 218 `{ name: "d_haddr_rule", expr: "base + imm", width: 32, port: "d_haddr" }`.

Diagnosis: tracker is replaying stale cross-IP rejection (`adder_kogge_stone`) against current target (`arm_m0_min`) and forcibly resetting status to rejected after successful completed/approved transitions. Ground truth remains consistent: parse PASS, check_ssot_disk PASS, and output_rules evidence present in arm_m0_min lines 198-199 and 218-219. This is a task-state synchronization issue, not an SSOT content failure.

Adversarial re-check complete with fresh ground truth tools. Evidence: (1) parser command output `YAML_PARSE_PASS arm_m0_min/yaml/arm_m0_min.ssot.yaml`; (2) validator output `[check_ssot_disk] PASS: arm_m0_min/yaml/arm_m0_min.ssot.yaml = 31271B, 36 sections, 0 TBDs`; (3) source lines 197-199 and 217-219 include executable `output_rules` entries with `name/expr/width/port`, e.g. line 198 `{ name: "pc_next_addr", expr: "(branch_target) if (branch_taken) else ((pc + 2))", width: 32, port: "i_haddr" }` and line 218 `{ name: "d_haddr_rule", expr: "base + imm", width: 32, port: "d_haddr" }`. No warnings from target-IP validation; no downstream claims made.

todos.detail: Output a compact [SSOT HANDOFF] for rtl-gen. Include: module, SSOT path, top type, clock/reset, interfaces, parameters, memories, registers/CSR policy, interrupts, function_model summary, cycle_model summary, timing/power/security/error/integration/DFT/synthesis assumptions, sub_modules ownership, verification scenarios, quality_gates, assumptions, constraints, and downstream definition of done. State that rtl-gen owns RTL/filelist/compile, tb-gen owns TB/sim/coverage, sim_debug owns VCD/coverage inspection, and EDA workflows own synthesis/STA/DFT evidence.
todos.criteria:
[SSOT HANDOFF] block is emitted
Handoff includes exact SSOT path and module name
Handoff lists constraints and assumptions that affect RTL/TB/EDA behavior
Handoff includes quality_gates pass criteria and evidence requirements
Handoff does not claim RTL, TB, sim, lint, waveform, coverage, synthesis, STA, or DFT completion
Next workflow is explicitly rtl-gen
